title: Chemically competent cells transformation
steps: Inoculate a single colony into 5 mL appropriate media containing appropriate antibiotics
Incubate overnight using appropriate temperature and shaking conditions
Dilute overnight culture to an optical density of 0.01 in 10 mL of appropriate media containing appropriate antibiotics
Incubate at appropriate temperature and shaking conditions until optical density reaches 0.4 to 0.6. Do not allow to grow past this point.
Spin down cells at 3500 RPM at 4°C for 10 min
Resuspend pellet in 10 mL ice-cold 0.1M CaCl 2
Place cells on ice for 20 min (do not go under this time; can go longer)
Spin down cells as in step 5.
Resuspend pellet in 1 mL ice-cold 0.1M CaCl 2 + 13% glycerol (for a higher concentration of cells, use less)
Place on ice for 20 min
Use immediately or store at -80°C (0.25 mL/tube)
Add 50 µL competent cells into a sterile Eppendorf tube
Add 1 µL plasmid DNA or ligation reaction (can vary this)
Place on ice for 30 min
Heat shock at 42°C for 1 min
Place on ice 2 min
Add 0.5 mL recovery media (SOC, LB, etc.)
Incubate for 1 hour at appropriate growth conditions.
Plate 200 µL on appropriate plate with appropriate antibiotics.
Incubate at 37°C overnight
creator: Eadewunm
